// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for environmental perception and object detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Telemetry sensor capturing vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuation sensor monitoring motor torque and related parameters for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationData
]

    base:desc "Actuation sensor monitoring steering angle and related parameters for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringActuationData
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor system processing object tracks and projected driving paths for ACC functionality"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw and processed LiDAR point cloud data transmitted for clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Telemetry data including speed, position, and orientation from vehicle state sensor"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor torque and actuation command data for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuatorSensor
]

instance SteeringActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering angle and actuation command data for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and projected driving paths derived from sensor fusion"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component processing raw LiDAR point clouds into structured clusters and tracks"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateNormalizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model normalizing and validating vehicle telemetry data for consistency"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateProcessingEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured LiDAR point cloud data after clustering and tracking preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized and validated vehicle state data ready for integration into 3D model"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and driving paths for ACC system visualization and control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ActuationCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized actuation commands for motor and steering systems"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationData, SteeringActuationData

]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based software component for LiDAR point cloud clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

]

instance EnvironmentalProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component processing object tracks and projected driving paths for ACC"

]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component ensuring serialized data alignment, unit conversions, and message standardization"

]

instance ROS2NetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 network enabling modular and interoperable data exchange between components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance WiFiUDPCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi with UDP protocol for low-latency, high-throughput physical-to-virtual data transmission"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance CloudEdgeComputingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

// C6: Services

]

]

    base:desc "Teleoperation and safety-critical control service enabling remote ACC management"
    DTDFVocab:provides AccControlInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction, SystemConfigurationAction
]

]

]

]

]

    base:desc "Computation of desired speed based on inter-vehicle distance for ACC"
]

    base:desc "Remote control interface for enabling/disabling ACC and setting desired speed"
]

]

    base:desc "Transmission of computed desired speed to physical twin for execution"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    base:desc "Remote issuance of emergency braking commands with real-time responsiveness"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Physical Twin Entity Components
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Software component processing raw LiDAR point clouds into structured clusters and tracks"
]

    base:desc "Component handling initial data preprocessing for sensor inputs"
]

    base:desc "Component ensuring serialized data alignment and message standardization"
]

    base:desc "Component storing processed data for historical analysis and reporting"
]

    base:desc "Component providing real-time 3D visualization of vehicle state and environmental data"
]

    base:desc "User interface component for ACC control and system configuration"
]

// C2: Acting Components

    base:desc "Actuator executing motor torque commands for vehicle propulsion"
]

]

// C12: Environments
instance ROS2NetworkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "ROS2 network environment for internal software component communication"

                 DataPreProcessingComponent, DataTranslationComponent
]

    base:desc "Wi-Fi UDP communication environment for physical-to-virtual data transmission"

]

instance CloudEdgeComputingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves control flows from the Digital Twin to the Physical Twin, governed by the following technical specifications:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Generated by the *Actuation FE* to control physical motors and servos (e.g., steering angle adjustments, throttle modulation).
   - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) Commands**: Derived from the *Controlling FE*, which processes projected driving paths and object tracks to issue speed/acceleration adjustments.
   - **User-Triggered Commands**: Inputs from the *User Interface FE* (e.g., control panel signals) translated into physical actions via the *Data Translation* component.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **ROS2 Messages**: Control commands are serialized into ROS2 message formats (e.g., `std_msgs/Float64` for steering angle, `geometry_msgs/Twist` for velocity) for transmission within the software stack.
   - **UDP Packets**: Inter-entity commands (e.g., from Digital Twin to Physical Twin) are encapsulated in UDP packets for Wi-Fi transmission, with payloads aligned to the *Data Translation* FE’s schema.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Data Translation FE**: Ensures command payloads adhere to predefined schemas, units, and safety thresholds (e.g., steering angle limits) before transmission.
   - **Legacy System Compatibility**: The *Data Translation* component handles translation between modern ROS2 formats and legacy systems (e.g., embedded motor controllers) via the *Legacy* interface.

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Closed-Loop Control**: Physical Twin sensors (e.g., motor encoders, LiDAR) provide real-time feedback to the Digital Twin via the *Data Monitoring* service, enabling adaptive adjustments (e.g., ACC recalibration).
   - **Visual Confirmation**: User interfaces (e.g., *3D Visualization*) display executed commands (e.g., steering angle, speed) for operator verification.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **Priority-Based Arbitration**: Conflicts between user inputs (e.g., manual steering override) and automated commands (e.g., ACC) are resolved via the *Controlling FE*, which enforces predefined priority rules (e.g., user input preempts ACC).
   - **Safety Checks**: The *Data Translation* component validates command sequences for physical feasibility (e.g., no simultaneous conflicting motor commands).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - **Acknowledgment Tokens**: Implicit confirmation via sensor feedback (e.g., motor encoder readings matching commanded torque) or explicit ROS2 service calls (e.g., `execute_command_ack`).
   - **Logging**: The *Reporting FE* records command execution timestamps, payloads, and outcomes for post-mortem analysis.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination. This temporal granularity is mandated to align with hardware limitations, ensuring deterministic behavior for safety-critical vehicular applications. While stricter clock synchronization (sub-100 ms) is required by some standards, the system adheres to this threshold due to practical constraints. The temporal aspects are further supported by the **ROS2 middleware**, which facilitates real-time communication between software components (nodes) deployed across distributed hardware (Functional Entities - FEs). Latency requirements are implicitly managed via the **entity-based architecture** (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User Entities), where data pre-processing, actuation, and device communication must operate within this 100 ms window to maintain coherence between the physical twin (e.g., sensors/actuators) and the digital twin (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) follows an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)-inspired, entity-based orchestration pattern**, where software components are mapped to **Functional Entities (FEs)** and deployed as **ROS2 packages** on distributed hardware. Key coordination mechanisms include:
  - **Component Integration**: Each FE (e.g., *Data Storage*, *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) is implemented as a ROS2 node, enabling loose coupling via topic-based or service-oriented communication. The *Physical Twin Entity* acts as a bridge between hardware resources (sensors/actuators) and higher-level abstractions.
  - **Resource Management**: The architecture explicitly separates compute, sensor, and actuator resources into the *Physical Twin Entity*, allowing dynamic allocation of functionalities (e.g., clustering) without violating ISO 23247’s modularity principles.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: The *Digital Twin Entity* serves as the central orchestrator for data management and control, while the *User Entity* provides a unified interface. Deviations from ISO 23247 (e.g., merging OME-like and general-purpose functionalities) are justified by hardware constraints and automotive-specific requirements, necessitating **cross-entity synchronization** via ROS2’s real-time capabilities."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard (Part 2) does not explicitly detail **fidelity requirements** for Digital Twin models or **validation/verification methods**, but it implies the following considerations:
  - **Model Fidelity**: The **Digital Twin Entity** must accurately represent the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, though the standard does not specify quantitative fidelity metrics (e.g., error thresholds, resolution requirements, or update frequencies). Fidelity is indirectly tied to the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**’s ability to capture high-quality, real-time data from the OME.
  - **Validation and Verification**: The standard lacks explicit methodologies for validating or verifying the Digital Twin’s accuracy, but the **Functional Entities (FEs)** within the Digital Twin Entity may include modules for cross-checking model outputs against physical OME behavior (e.g., via closed-loop control or simulation benchmarks). No specific techniques (e.g., model-in-the-loop, hardware-in-the-loop, or statistical validation) are prescribed.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**: Potential sources of uncertainty include **data inaccuracies** (e.g., sensor noise, transmission errors), **model approximations** (e.g., simplified physics or empirical correlations), and **temporal delays** in data synchronization between the Digital Twin and OME. The standard does not quantify these uncertainties or prescribe mitigation strategies.
  - **Error Detection and Correction**: The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** may implement mechanisms to detect anomalies (e.g., outliers in sensor data), but the standard does not specify correction protocols (e.g., automatic recalibration, user-triggered adjustments, or fallback modes). The **User Entity** could provide interfaces for manual intervention in error scenarios.
  - **Quality Assurance Processes**: The standard does not outline formal quality assurance (QA) processes, but the **entity-based architecture** suggests modular QA approaches, where each **Functional Entity** (e.g., data processing, model execution) undergoes independent validation. No specific QA frameworks (e.g., ISO 9001, Six Sigma) or tools (e.g., model validation suites) are referenced."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed with the following specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)**: Used for intra-entity (software component) communication, providing topics, services, and parameter servers for modular data exchange.
   - **Wi-Fi (UDP)**: Employed for inter-entity communication (Physical Twin → Digital Twin → User Entity) with UDP at the transport layer to minimize latency for real-time applications (e.g., ACC, collision avoidance).
   - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a middleware to serialize/deserialize data, align message types, and perform unit conversions (e.g., converting LiDAR point clouds to ROS2 `PointCloud2` messages).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Distributed ROS2 Network**: Software components (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, *Controlling FE*) communicate locally via ROS2 nodes and topics.
   - **Wi-Fi Backbone**: Physical Twin sensors (LiDAR, motor, steering) stream data to the Digital Twin over Wi-Fi, while user interfaces (e.g., *3D Visualization*) connect via the same network for control and monitoring.
   - **Hierarchical Deployment**: The architecture separates concerns into three entities (Physical, Digital, User) with clear data flow paths (Fig. 2).

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **LiDAR Data**: High-bandwidth streams (e.g., 64-point LiDAR scans at 10Hz+) require low-latency Wi-Fi to avoid visualization stutter or control delays.
   - **Actuation Commands**: Latency-critical commands (e.g., steering adjustments) must propagate within **<50ms** (implied by real-time ACC requirements).
   - **ROS2 Overhead**: Minimized via efficient message serialization and topic-based filtering (e.g., subscribing only to relevant data streams).

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **ROS2 Quality of Service (QoS)**: Configurable reliability policies (e.g., `RELIABLE` for actuation commands, `BEST_EFFORT` for visualization data) to balance latency and integrity.
   - **Data Translation Redundancy**: Critical data (e.g., vehicle state) is validated twice: once during preprocessing and again during translation to ensure consistency.
   - **Wi-Fi Resilience**: Not explicitly detailed, but implied to support real-time operations (e.g., no mention of packet loss handling suggests either low-latency Wi-Fi 6 or dedicated infrastructure).

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not in Document**: No explicit mention of encryption, authentication (e.g., TLS), or access control for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is characterized by the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: Implied by the **on-board** nature of sensors (LiDAR, motor, steering) and the real-time processing requirements (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* must run locally to avoid latency).
   - **On-Premise Digital Twin**: The Digital Twin entity (including *3D Visualization*, *Controlling FE*, and *Reporting FE*) likely resides on embedded or vehicle-integrated hardware (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, Raspberry Pi clusters) to minimize cloud dependency for latency-sensitive tasks.
   - **Cloud for Storage/Reporting**: The *Data Storage* and *Reporting FE* components suggest cloud or high-capacity on-premise storage for historical data and analytics.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **CPU/GPU**: High-performance compute for:
     - *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*: Likely GPU-accelerated (e.g., CUDA) for real-time LiDAR processing.
     - *3D Visualization*: Requires GPU rendering (e.g., OpenGL/Vulkan) for real-time 3D model updates.
   - **Memory**: Dynamic allocation for ROS2 nodes, with buffering for high-frequency sensor data (e.g., LiDAR point clouds).
   - **Storage**:
     - **Volatile**: RAM-based buffers for real-time data streams.
     - **Persistent**: Cloud/on-premise storage for logs, reports, and historical sensor data (via *Data Storage* component).

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Modular ROS2 Architecture**: Components can be scaled horizontally (e.g., deploying additional *Point Cloud Clustering* nodes for multi-LiDAR setups).
   - **Dynamic Topic Subscription**: ROS2’s publish-subscribe model allows scaling subscribers (e.g., adding more *Controlling FE* instances for fleet management).
   - **Legacy Integration**: The *Legacy* interface in *Data Translation* enables incremental scaling by supporting both modern and legacy hardware.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Hybrid Edge-Cloud**: Critical real-time processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering, actuation) occurs at the edge, while non-critical tasks (e.g., reporting) leverage cloud resources.
   - **Containerized Deployment**: Implied by ROS2’s compatibility with Docker/Kubernetes for portable, scalable component deployment.

5. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
   - **ROS2 Fault Tolerance**: Built-in mechanisms like node recovery and topic reconnection handle transient failures.
   - **Redundant Sensors**: Not explicitly mentioned, but real-time applications (e.g., ACC) likely rely on sensor fusion or fallback modes (e.g., camera-based tracking if LiDAR fails).
   - **Data Persistence**: Critical state data (e.g., vehicle position) is logged redundantly to ensure recovery from crashes.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based framework** for manufacturing Digital Twins. Key compliance aspects include:
  - **Entity-based Reference Architecture (RA)**: Mandates decomposition into **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, each with defined responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: Supports protocols like **OPC Unified Architecture** for data exchange between entities, ensuring seamless integration with industrial systems.
  - **Information Exchange**: Aligns with **ISO 23247 Part 4** for structured data interchange, though implementation specifics are not prescribed.
  - **Certification and Compliance**: The standard provides a **framework for certification** but does not detail specific certification requirements. The architecture inherently promotes **interoperability** and **modularity**, facilitating compliance with broader industrial standards."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to **ISO 26262-1:2018** for **functional safety** in road vehicles, which defines risk-based safety requirements for safety-critical systems, including **automotive Digital Twins**. This standard mandates **safety lifecycle processes**, **risk assessment methodologies (ASIL - Automotive Safety Integrity Levels)**, and **fail-safe mechanisms** to mitigate hazards in real-time systems. Additionally, **cybersecurity protocols** are implicitly required for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services as per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, which specifies **authentication systems** (e.g., digital signatures, cryptographic keys) and **access control mechanisms** to prevent unauthorized data manipulation or spoofing attacks. The **ISO 15622:2018** standard for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems** further enforces **threat mitigation strategies**, including **redundant sensor validation** and **real-time fault detection**, to ensure operational integrity. While **data ownership policies** and **privacy protection measures** (e.g., **consent management**, **data anonymization**) are not explicitly detailed in the provided documents, the context implies compliance with **EU GDPR** or equivalent regulations for **V2X data sharing agreements** in **3GPP TS 22.185**, though no granular technical specifications are provided."
]
